economic_notes: Nation achieved self-government status July 1, 2014 with recognized jurisdiction in over 50 areas. Operates gaming centre (Sioux Valley Gaming Centre), retail operations (Dakota Quickstop, Dakota Valley Gas Mart), and holdings entities. GAP funding restricted cash increased significantly from $9.39M to $20.09M.
notes: 14 entities fully consolidated including gaming, retail fuel, and housing operations. Long-term debt includes obligations under capital lease of $183,838 and long-term debt of $5,069,407. Current liabilities calculated as accounts payable $3,788,579 plus funding repayable $140,275. Total government transfers include ISC $56,433,154 + CMHC $908,515 + Southern First Nations Network of Care $3,933,420 + Canada Child Benefit $1,187,255 = $62,462,344. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Health Canada revenue shown as $0 in 2024 actual (budget was $2,944,134). Significant increase in restricted GAP funding cash from $9.39M to $20.09M. Band Amortization of $2,069,519 appears separately in expenses but total amortization per cash flow and capital asset note is $3,203,718. Expenses by segment include Capital schedule $3,247,050 and Band programs $7,967,832 not mapped to standard categories.
